_**User**_

Explore the SchaerbeekMunicipality codebase for current role/permission implementation:
- User roles (reception, population, police, admin)
- Authorization policies
- Who can view/edit registration cases
- CaseAuditAction or audit logging
- Any case assignment or locking mechanism

Key areas: Domain/Registration, Web/Features/Registration, auth/authorization setup

Return: current state of roles, what each role can do today, any existing audit/lock/assign code with file paths.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The full picture of the codebase is now clear.

No ASP.NET Core authentication exists. The system uses a demo ICurrentOfficer role switcher instead. The OfficerRole enum contains only three roles; Admin is absent. The outbound notifications page at /administration/ is restricted to PopulationOfficer via navigation, not an admin role. All roles can view every registration case without filtering. Edit permissions are determined by case status rather than role for intake operations.

Backend enforcement for approve, reject, suspend, resume, and confirm is restricted to the population officer through CanApproveRegistration. Police clerk UI blocks recording police results, yet the backend handler lacks any role check. Reception officers face no backend restrictions; the UI simply hides decision buttons because the section requires population officer approval rights.
</details></think>

## Executive summary

Authorization is **not real authentication**. The app uses a **demo role switcher** (`ICurrentOfficer`) with three roles. There is **no Admin role**, no `AddAuthentication`/`AddAuthorization`, and no ASP.NET authorization policies. Case editability is driven mainly by **case status**, not by who is assigned. **Officer-to-case locking does not exist**; assignment is informational/dashboard-only.

---

## Roles (current state)

Defined in `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Auth/OfficerRole.cs`:

| Role | Enum value | Demo identity |
|------|------------|---------------|
| Reception | `ReceptionOfficer` | Jean Martin (Reception) |
| Population | `PopulationOfficer` | Marie Dupont (Population) — **default** |
| Police | `PoliceClerk` | Luc Vermeulen (Police) |
| **Admin** | **Does not exist** | — |

**Auth setup files:**
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Auth/ICurrentOfficer.cs` — interface
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Auth/CurrentOfficer.cs` — scoped implementation; fixed `OfficerId` `11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Auth/Components/RoleSwitcher.razor` — UI dropdown in app bar
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Program.cs` — registers `ICurrentOfficer` only; **no auth middleware or policies**

**Only role-derived permission flag:** `CanApproveRegistration` → `true` only for `PopulationOfficer` (`CurrentOfficer.cs`).

---

## Authorization policies

**None.** Grep across the codebase finds no `Authorize`, `AddPolicy`, `RequireRole`, `UseAuthentication`, or `UseAuthorization`.

All `/api/registration/*` endpoints in `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/RegistrationEndpoints.cs` are **unauthenticated**. Enforcement is ad hoc in handlers and UI.

---

## What each role can do today

### Reception officer (`ReceptionOfficer`)
- **View:** All cases, case detail, audit timeline, documents (same as other roles).
- **Edit intake:** Yes, when case status is `Intake` or `UnderReview` (no role check on intake handlers).
- **Open cases:** Yes (`OpenRegistrationCaseHandler` — assigns to current officer by default).
- **Send to police:** Yes (UI + handler; no role gate).
- **Approve / reject / suspend / resume / confirm registration:** **No** — buttons hidden (`CaseDecisionSection.razor`); handlers throw `UnauthorizedAccessException`.
- **Record police results:** UI button disabled; backend would still accept the API call.
- **Issue certificates:** Yes if case is `Registered` (no role check).
- **Nav:** Home, Review dashboard, Registration cases (Police verifications and Outbound notifications hidden).

### Population officer (`PopulationOfficer`)
- Everything Reception can do, **plus:**
- **Approve, reject, suspend, resume, confirm registration** — enforced via `CanApproveRegistration` in:
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/ApproveCase/ApproveCaseHandler.cs`
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/RejectCase/RejectCaseHandler.cs`
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/SuspendCase/SuspendCaseHandler.cs`
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/ResumeCase/ResumeCaseHandler.cs`
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/ConfirmRegistration/ConfirmRegistrationHandler.cs`
- **Outbound notifications page** — nav item restricted to `PopulationOfficer` (`MainLayout.razor`).

### Police clerk (`PoliceClerk`)
- **View:** All cases; police verification list (nav item restricted to `PoliceClerk`).
- **Record police results:** UI enabled on `PoliceVerificationList.razor`; handler `RecordPoliceResultHandler.cs` has **no role check**.
- **Edit intake / decisions / certificates:** Same technical access as Reception (status-gated only); decision UI still hidden because `CanApproveRegistration` is false.

### Admin
**Not implemented.** The `/administration/outbound-notifications` route is a **population-only nav item**, not an admin role.

---

## Who can view / edit registration cases

### View
- **Any role, any case** — `ListRegistrationCasesHandler.cs` and `GetRegistrationCaseHandler.cs` have no role or assignment filters.

### Edit (intake data)
Gated by **status**, not role or assignee:

**Domain rule** — `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs`:

```325:332:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs
    public void EnsureIntakeDataEditable(string operation)
    {
        if (Status is not (RegistrationCaseStatus.Intake or RegistrationCaseStatus.UnderReview))
        {
            throw new InvalidRegistrationTransitionException(
                $"Cannot perform '{operation}' while the case is in status '{Status}'.");
        }
    }
```

**UI mirror** — `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseDetail.razor`:

```329:331:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/RegistrationCaseDetail.razor
    private bool CanEditIntakeData =>
        _case is not null &&
        _case.Status is RegistrationCaseStatus.Intake or RegistrationCaseStatus.UnderReview;
```

**Status editability matrix:**

| Status | Intake editable? | Notes |
|--------|------------------|-------|
| `Intake` | Yes | |
| `UnderReview` | Yes | Corrections allowed (ADR-0004) |
| `AwaitingPoliceVerification` | **No** | Police-send “lock” |
| `Approved` | No | Decision actions only (population) |
| `Registered` | No | Certificates only |
| `Rejected` | No | Terminal |
| `Suspended` | No | Resume only (population) |

Handlers calling `EnsureIntakeDataEditable` include identity, address, household, civil status, documents, BIS conversion, etc. under `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/`.

---

## Case assignment

**Exists as metadata only — not enforced for access control.**

| Piece | Path |
|-------|------|
| Domain field | `RegistrationCase.AssignedOfficerId` — `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs` |
| Set on open | `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/OpenRegistrationCase/OpenRegistrationCaseHandler.cs` — uses `AssignedOfficerId` from request or `ICurrentOfficer.OfficerId` |
| Exposed in DTO | `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/GetRegistrationCase/RegistrationCaseDetailDto.cs` (`AssignedOfficerId`) |
| Dashboard use | `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/GetReviewDashboard/GetReviewDashboardHandler.cs` — “My open cases” counts cases where `AssignedOfficerId == currentOfficer.OfficerId` |

**No reassignment endpoint.** **No check** that only the assigned officer may edit.

**Decision officer** (separate): `DecisionOfficerId` set on approve/reject/suspend in domain; stored but not used for access control.

---

## Locking mechanism

**No officer-level lock.** Only **workflow/status locking**:

1. **Police verification lock** — sending to police sets `AwaitingPoliceVerification`, which blocks `EnsureIntakeDataEditable`. Documented in UI consequence text (`RegistrationCaseDetail.razor` line ~598) and `docs/features/registration/request-police-verification.md`.
2. **Terminal/post-decision lock** — `Approved`, `Registered`, `Rejected`, `Suspended` block intake edits via the same domain method.

There is no `IsLocked`, `LockedByOfficerId`, lock release, or admin unlock in the codebase.

---

## Audit logging (`CaseAuditAction`)

### Domain model
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/CaseAuditAction.cs` — 13 enum values
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/CaseAuditEntry.cs`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/ICaseAuditRepository.cs`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/Persistence/Repositories/CaseAuditRepository.cs`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/CaseAuditEntryConfiguration.cs`
- DB table: `case_audit_entries` (migration `20260705113833_DecisionAndRegistration.cs`)

### Recorder
`src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/CaseAuditRecorder.cs` — stamps `ICurrentOfficer.OfficerId` and timestamp.

### **Recorded today** (7 of 13 actions)

| Action | Handler |
|--------|---------|
| `CaseApproved` | `ApproveCaseHandler.cs` |
| `CaseRejected` | `RejectCaseHandler.cs` |
| `CaseSuspended` | `SuspendCaseHandler.cs` |
| `CaseResumed` | `ResumeCaseHandler.cs` |
| `RegistrationConfirmed` | `ConfirmRegistrationHandler.cs` |
| `CertificateIssued` | `IssueResidenceCertificateHandler.cs`, `IssueHouseholdCompositionHandler.cs` |

### **Defined but not recorded**
`CaseOpened`, `IdentityRecorded`, `IdentityCorrected`, `ResidenceCategorySet`, `AddressDeclared`, `PoliceVerificationRequested`, `PoliceResultRecorded` — no `auditRecorder.RecordAsync` calls in intake or police handlers.

### Read/display
- API: `GET /api/registration/cases/{id}/audit` — `ListCaseAuditEndpoint.cs` / `ListCaseAuditHandler.cs`
- UI: case detail timeline — `RegistrationCaseDetail.razor` loads audit via `ListCaseAuditHandler`
- Tests: `tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CaseDecisionTests.cs`

---

## Role-based UI (nav & gates)

| Location | Behavior |
|----------|----------|
| `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/DesignSystem/Components/Navigation/AppSideNavigation.razor` | Filters nav by `AppNavItem.RequiredRole` |
| `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` | Police verifications → `PoliceClerk`; Outbound notifications → `PopulationOfficer` |
| `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Pages/PoliceVerificationList.razor` | Alert + disabled button unless `PoliceClerk` |
| `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/CaseDecisionSection.razor` | `CanDecide` requires `CanApproveRegistration` |

---

## Gaps vs. your role list

| Expected | Current |
|----------|---------|
| Reception | `ReceptionOfficer` — exists, minimal differentiation |
| Population | `PopulationOfficer` — decision authority |
| Police | `PoliceClerk` — UI-only gate on police list |
| Admin | **Missing** |
| Authorization policies | **Missing** |
| Officer case lock | **Missing** (only status-based lock) |
| Full audit trail | **Partial** — decisions + certificates only |

If you want officer-level locking, admin unlock, or full intake audit, that would be new work on top of this foundation — switch to Agent mode if you’d like it implemented.
